Contradicted. Ran R `circular` package code. Mean direction of the control group ('c' treatment) is ~6.32°. The 10-30° range in metadata and verifier logic will falsely reject correct submissions.
Verified. Ran R `circular` package code. The mean resultant length (rho) for the control group is ~0.745, correctly satisfying the > 0.5 minimum condition.
Verified. Ran R `circular` package code. A Rayleigh test of uniformity yields p < 0.0001, proving significance.
Verified. Ran R `circular` package code. A Watson-Williams test comparing the control group ('c') to experimental groups ('v1', 'on') yields a p-value > 0.05, denoting non-significance.

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for rstudio_env pigeon_orientation_circular task by analyzing the real 'pigeons' homing dataset from the 'circular' package. I discovered two major flaws: 1) The prompt falsely claims the dataset is a list with '$control' and '$experimental' components, but it is actually a 'data.frame' with columns 'treatment' (c, on, v1) and 'bearing'. 2) The actual mean direction for the control group ('c') is ~6.32°, completely missing the claimed metadata target of [10, 30] and the flawed verifier.py constraint of 10-40°. The expected Rho (>0.5), Rayleigh test significance (p < 0.05), and Watson-Williams non-significance (p > 0.05) claims were empirically verified using R code execution.
